Class Worklist

java.lang.Object
recoder.util.Worklist
public class Worklist extends Object
this class is a simple wrapper for the data structure Queue, which provides slightly more functionality, such as avoiding duplicates or building length statistics.

  • Field Summary

    Fields
    Modifier and Type
    Field
    Description
    protected final boolean
    allowDuplicates
    should it be possible to add items to the worklist twice?
    protected int
    maxLen
    records thw maximum length of the worklist

  • Field Details

    • allowDuplicates

      protected final boolean allowDuplicates
      should it be possible to add items to the worklist twice?

    • maxLen

      protected int maxLen
      records thw maximum length of the worklist

  • Constructor Details

    • Worklist

      public Worklist()
      creates a new empty worklist

    • Worklist

      public Worklist(boolean allowDuplicates)
      creates a new empty worklist.
      Parameters:
      allowDuplicates - indicates whether or not to allow duplicate items

  • Method Details

    • isEmpty

      public final boolean isEmpty()
      determines whether the worklist is empty or not.

    • addItem

      public void addItem(Object todo)
      adds the given item to the worklist. If the worklist does not allow duplicates and the item is already contained, it is ignored.
      Parameters:
      todo - the item to be added

    • addItem

      public void addItem(Object todo, boolean allowDuplicates)
      adds the given item to the worklist, overriding the default duplicate handling strategy.
      Parameters:
      todo - the item to be added
      allowDuplicates - indicates whether there may be duplicates of the item or not

    • getItem

      public Object getItem()
      retrieves the first item from the worklist
      Returns:
      the first item of the list or null if the list is empty

    • removeItem

      public Object removeItem()
      removes the first item from the worklist and returns the removed item. If the worklist is empty, the method returns null.
      Returns:
      the removed item (the former first item) or null if the list was empty

    • contains

      public boolean contains(Object x)
      determines whether the worklist contains some element
      Returns:
      true if there is at least one element in the list

    • elements

      public Enumeration<Object> elements()
      returns an enumeration of the elements within the list.
      Returns:
      a java.util.Enumeration object

    • size

      public int size()
      determines the number of items in the worklist.
      Returns:
      the size of the worklist

    • getMaximumLength

      public int getMaximumLength()
      returns the (historical) maximum length of the list
